deck_decisions: Drakloak is raised from 2 to 4 and Dragapult ex reduced from 4 to 3. Recon Directive is a repeatable draw/filter engine, while Tera Orb and Dawn already provide direct Dragapult access.
Duskull is reduced from 4 to 3. One live Cursed Blast line is required, but four Duskull overfills the five-slot Bench shared with two Dragapult lines, Azelf, and the Cinderace handoff.
Psychic Energy is reduced from 8 to 7, preserving thirteen total Energy. Cinderace and Crispin accelerate colored requirements, while the policy rejects redundant attachments.
Dawn is raised to 3 because one Supporter simultaneously finds a Basic, Stage 1, and Stage 2 and can assemble either complete evolution line.
Night Stretcher is raised to 2 because Cursed Blast self-KOs and the deck must recover Pokémon or the missing basic Energy color without discarding two cards.
Prime Catcher remains the ACE SPEC because it performs the exact Cinderace-to-Dragapult handoff while gusting the intended Prize target.
opening_plan: Use Cinderace as the setup Active when offered.
Buddy-Buddy Poffin prefers Dreepy plus Duskull; a second Dreepy is next priority and Azelf is delayed until the damage-counter reservoir matters.
Turbo Flare attaches only colors that complete real Dragapult routes and does not place redundant Energy on a ready attacker.
midgame_plan: Keep one or more Drakloak in play when immediate Phantom Dive is unavailable; every Recon Directive is a separate once-per-turn draw/filter activation.
Rare Candy priority is attack-ready Dragapult, immediate positive-prize Dusknoir route, backup Dragapult, then Cinderace recovery.
Phantom Dive's six counters are assigned one at a time: immediate KO, Mega/ex Prize target, entry into the 130/50 Cursed Blast bands, then lowest remaining HP.
Dusclops and Dusknoir are not activated merely because legal. Their self-KO must take a Prize, enable the active Dragapult KO, or create an Azelf terminal.
endgame_plan: Prime Catcher and Switch move a ready Dragapult out from behind Cinderace before low-value setup actions.
Boss's Orders is reserved for a confirmed Prize conversion.
Azelf is powered only when Neurokinesis converts the total opposing damage-counter reservoir into a credible one-Prize finish.

required_captures: Turbo Flare Energy and Bench target
Phantom Dive six repeated DamageCounterAny targets
Crispin hand Energy and attachment target
